lines.english: Lesson 23
lines.chinese: 第23课
lines.raw: Lesson 23 | 第23课

lines.english: Bird flight
lines.chinese: 鸟的飞行
lines.raw: Bird flight | 鸟的飞行

lines.english: What are the two main types of bird flight described by the author?
lines.chinese: 作者描述的鸟类飞行的两种主要类型是什么？
lines.raw: What are the two main types of bird flight described by the author? | 作者描述的鸟类飞行的两种主要类型是什么？

lines.english: No two sorts of birds practise quite the same sort of flight;
lines.chinese: 没有两种鸟的飞行方式是完全相同的；
lines.raw: No two sorts of birds practise quite the same sort of flight; | 没有两种鸟的飞行方式是完全相同的；

lines.english: the varieties are infinite; but two classes may be roughly seen.
lines.chinese: 种类是无限的；但大致可分为两类。
lines.raw: the varieties are infinite; but two classes may be roughly seen. | 种类是无限的；但大致可分为两类。

lines.english: Any ship that crosses the Pacific
lines.chinese: 任何一艘横渡太平洋的船只
lines.raw: Any ship that crosses the Pacific | 任何一艘横渡太平洋的船只

lines.english: is accompanied for many days by the smaller albatross,
lines.chinese: 会有较小的信天翁伴随数日，
lines.raw: is accompanied for many days by the smaller albatross, | 会有较小的信天翁伴随数日，

lines.english: which may keep company with the vessel for an hour
lines.chinese: 它们可以陪伴船只一个小时
lines.raw: which may keep company with the vessel for an hour | 它们可以陪伴船只一个小时

lines.english: without visible or more than occasional movement of wing.
lines.chinese: 翅膀几乎不动或只是偶尔动一下。
lines.raw: without visible or more than occasional movement of wing. | 翅膀几乎不动或只是偶尔动一下。

lines.english: The currents of air that the walls of the ship direct upwards,
lines.chinese: 船体向上引导的气流，
lines.raw: The currents of air that the walls of the ship direct upwards, | 船体向上引导的气流，

lines.english: as well as in the line of its course,
lines.chinese: 以及沿其航线的气流，
lines.raw: as well as in the line of its course, | 以及沿其航线的气流，

lines.english: are enough to give the great bird with its immense wings
lines.chinese: 足以为这种有着巨大翅膀的大鸟
lines.raw: are enough to give the great bird with its immense wings | 足以为这种有着巨大翅膀的大鸟

lines.english: sufficient sustenance and progress.
lines.chinese: 提供足够的升力和前进动力。
lines.raw: sufficient sustenance and progress. | 提供足够的升力和前进动力。

lines.english: The albatross is the king of the gliders,
lines.chinese: 信天翁是滑翔之王，
lines.raw: The albatross is the king of the gliders, | 信天翁是滑翔之王，

lines.english: the class of fliers which harness the air to their purpose,
lines.chinese: 这类飞行者利用空气达到目的，
lines.raw: the class of fliers which harness the air to their purpose, | 这类飞行者利用空气达到目的，

lines.english: but must yield to its opposition.
lines.chinese: 但也必须屈服于空气的阻力。
lines.raw: but must yield to its opposition. | 但也必须屈服于空气的阻力。

lines.english: In the contrary school, the duck is supreme.
lines.chinese: 在相反的学派中，鸭子是至高无上的。
lines.raw: In the contrary school, the duck is supreme. | 在相反的学派中，鸭子是至高无上的。

lines.english: It comes nearer to the engines with which man has 'conquered' the air, as he boasts.
lines.chinese: 它更接近于人类声称“征服”天空的发动机。
lines.raw: It comes nearer to the engines with which man has 'conquered' the air, as he boasts. | 它更接近于人类声称“征服”天空的发动机。

lines.english: Duck, and like them the pigeons, are endowed with steel-like muscles,
lines.chinese: 鸭子，以及像它们一样的鸽子，都拥有钢铁般的肌肉，
lines.raw: Duck, and like them the pigeons, are endowed with steel-like muscles, | 鸭子，以及像它们一样的鸽子，都拥有钢铁般的肌肉，

lines.english: that are a good part of the weight of the bird,
lines.chinese: 这些肌肉占据了鸟类体重很大一部分，
lines.raw: that are a good part of the weight of the bird, | 这些肌肉占据了鸟类体重很大一部分，

lines.english: and these will ply the short wings with such irresistible power
lines.chinese: 它们以不可抗拒的力量挥动短小的翅膀，
lines.raw: and these will ply the short wings with such irresistible power | 它们以不可抗拒的力量挥动短小的翅膀，

lines.english: that they can bore for long distances through an opposing gale
lines.chinese: 可以在逆风中飞行很长的距离
lines.raw: that they can bore for long distances through an opposing gale | 可以在逆风中飞行很长的距离

lines.english: before exhaustion follows.
lines.chinese: 而不致精疲力尽。
lines.raw: before exhaustion follows. | 而不致精疲力尽。

lines.english: Their humbler followers, such as partridges,
lines.chinese: 它们较小的追随者，例如鹧鸪，
lines.raw: Their humbler followers, such as partridges, | 它们较小的追随者，例如鹧鸪，

lines.english: have a like power of strong propulsion, but soon tire.
lines.chinese: 也有类似的强大推进力，但很快就会疲劳。
lines.raw: have a like power of strong propulsion, but soon tire. | 也有类似的强大推进力，但很快就会疲劳。

lines.english: You may pick them up in utter exhaustion,
lines.chinese: 你可能会发现它们筋疲力尽，
lines.raw: You may pick them up in utter exhaustion, | 你可能会发现它们筋疲力尽，

lines.english: if wind over the sea has driven them to a long journey.
lines.chinese: 如果海上的风迫使它们长途跋涉。
lines.raw: if wind over the sea has driven them to a long journey. | 如果海上的风迫使它们长途跋涉。

lines.english: The swallow shares the virtues of both schools in highest measure.
lines.chinese: 燕子则兼具这两种飞行的优点，而且达到了最高境界。
lines.raw: The swallow shares the virtues of both schools in highest measure. | 燕子则兼具这两种飞行的优点，而且达到了最高境界。

lines.english: It tires not, nor does it boast of its power; but belongs to the air
lines.chinese: 它不疲倦，也不炫耀自己的力量；它属于天空，
lines.raw: It tires not, nor does it boast of its power; but belongs to the air | 它不疲倦，也不炫耀自己的力量；它属于天空，

lines.english: travelling it may be six thousand miles to and from its northern nesting home,
lines.chinese: 也许飞行六千英里往返于它的北方巢穴，
lines.raw: travelling it may be six thousand miles to and from its northern nesting home, | 也许飞行六千英里往返于它的北方巢穴，

lines.english: feeding its flown young as it flies, and slipping through a medium
lines.chinese: 边飞边喂养已经出巢的幼鸟，滑过介质
lines.raw: feeding its flown young as it flies, and slipping through a medium | 边飞边喂养已经出巢的幼鸟，滑过介质

lines.english: that seems to help its passage even when the wind is adverse.
lines.chinese: 即使逆风，这介质似乎也在帮助它通过。
lines.raw: that seems to help its passage even when the wind is adverse. | 即使逆风，这介质似乎也在帮助它通过。

lines.english: Such birds do us good,
lines.chinese: 这样的鸟对我们有益，
lines.raw: Such birds do us good, | 这样的鸟对我们有益，

lines.english: though we no longer take omens from their flight on this side and that;
lines.chinese: 尽管我们不再从它们飞行的方向来卜吉凶；
lines.raw: though we no longer take omens from their flight on this side and that; | 尽管我们不再从它们飞行的方向来卜吉凶；

lines.english: and even the most superstitious villagers no longer take off their hats to the magpie and wish it good-morning.
lines.chinese: 甚至最迷信的村民也不再向喜鹊脱帽致意并祝它早上好。
lines.raw: and even the most superstitious villagers no longer take off their hats to the magpie and wish it good-morning. | 甚至最迷信的村民也不再向喜鹊脱帽致意并祝它早上好。
